ECG diagnosis of STEMI in a setting of preceding LBBB is difficult, but the following are useful: ST changes in the same direction, ST segment elevation > 7-8 mm, Coving of ST segment, Pathological Q wave in 2 consecutive leads and Reciprocal changes

Cardiac enzymes: Cardiac troponin I & T are released within 4 hours of MI and remain elevated for up to 2 weeks, It is the most sensitive marker of myocardial damage. Useful in late presentation, MB-CK rises within 4-6 hours, peaks in a day and disappears by the second day
